findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info is used for runtime column detection (canonical_file_path, document_type). Turso/libSQL supports PRAGMA but column introspection semantics may differ.

findings.signature: SELECT file_path, canonical_file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash, embedding_status FROM memory_index WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ? ORDER BY CASE WHEN canonical_file_path = ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id DESC LIMIT 1
findings.notes: memory_index table accessed with CASE expression in ORDER BY — standard SQL, Turso-compatible. Table schema assumed created elsewhere.
SQLite datetime('now') used in UPDATE SET. Turso/libSQL supports this built-in.
better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper. Turso/libSQL JS client uses async transactions — this pattern requires rewrite.
Type union references 'fts' and 'bm25' as sink targets. No direct SQL but shapes the write-path routing — migration must preserve these target semantics.

findings.signature: SELECT source_id, target_id, relation FROM causal_edges WHERE relation IN ('contradicts','supersedes') AND source_id IN (?) AND target_id IN (?)
findings.notes: Reads causal_edges table with parameterized IN clauses. Standard SQL, Turso-compatible. Table DDL assumed elsewhere.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mutation_ledger (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ','now')), mutation_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(...), reason TEXT NOT NULL, prior_hash TEXT, new_hash TEXT NOT NULL, linked_memory_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', decision_meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', actor TEXT NOT NULL, session_id TEXT)
findings.notes: Full DDL with AUTOINCREMENT, strftime default, CHECK constraint, and JSON text columns. Turso supports all but verify AUTOINCREMENT semantics.

findings.signature: CREATE INDEX idx_ledger_memory_type_created_at ON mutation_ledger(CAST(json_extract(linked_memory_ids, '$[0]') AS INTEGER), mutation_type, timestamp)
findings.notes: Expression index using json_extract on JSON array element. Turso/libSQL lacks json_extract in index expressions — this index cannot be created as-is.

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
findings.notes: BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers enforce append-only immutability. Turso/libSQL lacks CREATE TRIGGER — this enforcement must move to application code.

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
findings.notes: DELETE trigger for append-only enforcement. Turso/libSQL lacks triggers — must be replaced with application-level guard.

findings.signature: SELECT ml.* FROM mutation_ledger ml, json_each(ml.linked_memory_ids) je WHERE je.value = ?
findings.notes: json_each() table-valued function used to expand JSON arrays for JOIN queries. Turso/libSQL lacks json_each — must rewrite as application-side JSON parsing.
json_extract in WHERE clause for query filtering. Turso supports json_extract in queries but not in indexes.

findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'trigger' AND tbl_name = 'mutation_ledger'
findings.notes: Queries sqlite_master for trigger verification. Turso/libSQL has sqlite_master but triggers don't exist — verifyAppendOnly will always return false.
strftime with millisecond precision in column default. Turso/libSQL supports strftime but verify %f (fractional seconds) behavior.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO memory_idempotency_receipts (...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET response_payload = excluded.response_payload, memory_id = excluded.memory_id, updated_at = datetime('now')
findings.notes: Upsert with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Turso/libSQL supports upsert — this is migration-safe.

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = ?, last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?
findings.notes: Writes JSON hint to near_duplicate_of column. Standard UPDATE with datetime — Turso-compatible.
Dynamic IN clause with parameterized placeholders. Standard SQL pattern, Turso-compatible.
Synchronous transaction wrapper from transaction-manager.js. Turso/libSQL async client requires await-based transactions.
